other_observers: There was a group from Denver whom I helped get onto the bird. I did not know any of them.; I was the original finder.
description: Not a very bright individual.  Gray crown, bordered by white supercilium that extended from the base of the bill to past the eye. Gray line through eye. Yellow on underside was soft, extended from breast, down flank to the under tail. Gray/green above. No wing bars. Gray bare parts (bill and legs).Behaviors: Alone. Working wild rose bushes at ground level up to a couple of feet above ground. Actively feeding.  The area was full of migrants (warblers, thrushes, sparrows, empids, etc.). This was the only individual of this species I saw. Nicely enough, there was a Tennessee Warbler directly above it, about 12-15 feet off the ground feeding in the trees. This gave for a great comparison.Call: silentPlumage: Adult
